content.problemStatement:
You are given a sorted array numsnumsnums of distinct positive integers in **strictly increasing order** and a number kkk.

Your task is to determine the kthk^\text{th}kth **smallest positive integer** that does **not appear** in the array.

Function DeclarationFunction NamefindKthPositivefindKthPositivefindKthPositive – This function finds the kthk^\text{th}kth missing positive integer from a sorted array.

Parameters
numsnumsnums : A sorted array of distinct positive integers.
kkk : An integer representing the position of the missing positive number to find.
Return Value
Returns a single integer — the kthk^\text{th}kth **missing positive number**.
Constraints
1≤T≤1001 \leq T \leq 1001≤T≤100
1≤nums.length≤10001 \leq nums.length \leq 10001≤nums.length≤1000
1≤nums[i]≤100001 \leq nums[i] \leq 100001≤nums[i]≤10000
1≤k≤100001 \leq k \leq 100001≤k≤10000
nums[i]<nums[j]nums[i] < nums[j]nums[i]<nums[j] for all i<ji < ji<j
Array is strictly increasing

Input Format

The first line contains the number of test case TTT 

Each test case contains:

The first line contains two integers NNN and kkk — the size of the array and the missing index.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers representing the array numsnumsnums.




Output Format

Print a single integer — the kthk^\text{th}kth **missing positive number**.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains two integers N and k — the size of the array and the required missing index. The second line contains N space‑separated distinct positive integers in strictly increasing order.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the k‑th missing positive integer.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: missing numbers are 1,2,4,7,8,9,... The 4th missing is 7. Second test case: missing numbers are 2,3,6,7,9,10,... The 6th missing is 10.
Missing numbers start from 1. The 5th missing number is 5.

solution.approach: For each position i (0‑based) in the array the number of missing positive integers before nums[i] equals nums[i] - (i + 1). Scan the array from left to right. If this missing count becomes >= k, the answer lies before nums[i] and is exactly k + i. If the loop finishes, all missing numbers are after the last element, so the answer is k + N. This runs in O(N) time per test case and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the array.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space besides the input array.
